artPrompt: A weathered carnival performer flexing in a tattered vest, his skin a dense map of inked dice, coins, and probability symbols crowding every inch of bare arm and chest, striking a showman's pose under hot gold spotlight amid striped tents and ember sparks. Every tattoo a probability he survived, running out of skin. The figure dominates the central wide frame, firelight below. Wide 4:3 landscape, carnival red and smoky black, sinister festivity, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: The Tattooed Man of Statistics
text: When The Tattooed Man of Statistics enters the battlefield, the table tallies every die rolled and coin flipped so far this game (best guess; do not actually keep a log, just argue about it for ten seconds). You draw a card for every fifth roll or flip in that total, rounded however the loudest player insists.
flavorText: Every tattoo is a probability he survived. He is running out of skin.

extras.fourthWall: estimate the game's total rolls/flips by table argument
